original_hypothesis.title: ETH Deposit Fee Deposit Re-Processed with Fee Deduction - Systematic Proposer Fee Leakage
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In LibDepositing.processDeposits(), the accumulated totalFee is written as a new deposit entry in the ETH deposit ring buffer at index numEthDeposits. This fee deposit is treated identically to a user deposit and will be processed by the NEXT block's processDeposits call, where a processing fee is deducted from it. This means the original proposer (fee recipient) receives less than the full fee on L2, with the difference going to the next block's proposer as processing revenue.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This creates a systematic ~3-12% fee leakage per block depending on gas conditions. Over the protocol's lifetime, this represents significant misallocation of ETH deposit processing fees between proposers.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Fee deposit written to ethDeposits[numEthDeposits % ringBufferSize] with numEthDeposits++; this entry falls within the processable range for next block, causing recursive fee deduction

preconditions: ETH deposits are being processed (numPending >= ethDepositMinCountPerBlock = 8)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Fee deposit at index numEthDeposits becomes a processable deposit for next block; fee = min(ethDepositMaxFee, basefee * ethDepositGas) deducted from it
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Not attacker-controlled but systematically affects all proposers. A strategic proposer could time their blocks to capture other proposers' fee deposits.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Per block loss: fee ≈ basefee * 21000 ≈ 0.001 ETH at 50 Gwei. If totalFee ≈ 0.01-0.03 ETH, loss is ~3-10%

economic_questions: What is the cumulative fee leakage across all proposed blocks?
Is this an intentional design choice or an accounting bug?
Can a proposer strategically time blocks to capture more fee deposits from other proposers?
validation_requirements: Trace a complete fee deposit lifecycle through two consecutive processDeposits calls
Calculate the exact amount lost per block under various basefee conditions
Verify the L2 credit amount matches expectations

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: ~0.001 ETH per block captured by next proposer
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: 3-12% of fee deposits leaked to wrong recipient

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: High basefee periods maximize absolute fee deduction
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Systematic undercompensation of proposers for deposit processing

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All block proposers who process ETH deposits
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: ~0.001 ETH per block under normal gas conditions
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Cumulative across all blocks with processed deposits

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Simulate two consecutive processDeposits: verify fee deposit from block N appears in block N+1 processing

validation_result.mechanism: fee deposit re-processed as regular deposit
validation_result.impact_summary: The totalFee accumulated during processDeposits is written back into the ETH deposit ring buffer and will eventually be re-processed as a regular deposit, causing a processing fee to be deducted from it. This results in systematic fee leakage from one proposer (fee recipient) to subsequent proposers.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
In LibDepositing.processDeposits(), after processing user deposits and accumulating totalFee, the function writes the fee deposit at index numEthDeposits in the ring buffer (line 95-96) and increments numEthDeposits (line 101). This fee deposit entry falls into the range [nextEthDepositToProcess, numEthDeposits) for future processDeposits calls. When enough pending deposits accumulate (>= ethDepositMinCountPerBlock = 8), this fee deposit will be processed identically to a user deposit, with a processing fee deducted from it (line 86: `_fee = deposits_[i].amount > fee ? fee : deposits_[i].amount`). The original fee recipient receives less than the full fee on L2, with the difference going to the next block's proposer as processing revenue.

The fee deposit is not immediately processed in the next block since ethDepositMinCountPerBlock = 8 and only one fee deposit is pending. However, as user deposits and additional fee deposits accumulate across multiple blocks, they will eventually be batch-processed. At that point, each fee deposit has a processing fee deducted from it.

At 50 Gwei basefee, fee per deposit = min(0.1 ETH, 50e9 * 21000) = 0.00105 ETH. For 8 deposits processed: totalFee ≈ 0.0084 ETH. When this fee deposit is later processed, 0.00105 ETH is deducted, meaning the fee recipient loses ~12.5% of their fee.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Lines 83-101 of LibDepositing.sol clearly show: (1) fee deposit written to ethDeposits[numEthDeposits % ringBufferSize] at line 95-96, (2) numEthDeposits incremented at line 101, (3) nextEthDepositToProcess advanced only past user deposits at line 94. This places the fee deposit squarely in the pending range for future processing. Lines 77-93 show the loop that processes deposits with fee deduction, which will apply equally to fee deposits when they come up for processing.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The fee deposit is written into the same ring buffer that processDeposits reads from. After writing, numEthDeposits is incremented but nextEthDepositToProcess has already been advanced past only the user deposits. The fee deposit at the new numEthDeposits-1 index becomes a pending deposit that will be processed in a future call. When processed, it will have a processing fee deducted just like any user deposit, reducing the fee recipient's L2 credit.

calling_sequence: 3. processDeposits processes user deposits, deducting fees (lines 83-93)
5. Fee deposit written at numEthDeposits index (lines 95-96)
7. Fee deposit is now in pending range: numEthDeposits > nextEthDepositToProcess
8. In future proposeBlock call, processDeposits processes fee deposit as regular deposit
9. Processing fee deducted from fee deposit amount (line 86)
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: Block N: processDeposits processes deposits [nextEthDepositToProcess, nextEthDepositToProcess + count). Sets nextEthDepositToProcess += count. Writes fee deposit at numEthDeposits. Increments numEthDeposits. Now pending = numEthDeposits - nextEthDepositToProcess includes the fee deposit. Block N+k (when enough pending >= 8): processDeposits iterates through pending deposits including the fee deposit from Block N. Fee is deducted: _fee = min(fee, feeDeposit.amount). Fee recipient on L2 receives feeDeposit.amount - _fee instead of feeDeposit.amount.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Config values from TaikoL1.getConfig(): ethDepositMinCountPerBlock=8, ethDepositMaxCountPerBlock=32, ethDepositGas=21000, ethDepositMaxFee=0.1 ether, ethDepositRingBufferSize=1024. These are hardcoded.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an active attack but a systematic protocol defect. Every time deposits are processed, the fee deposit goes back into the ring buffer. When it is eventually processed (along with enough other pending deposits to meet the minimum threshold of 8), a fee is deducted. At 50 Gwei basefee: per-deposit fee = 0.00105 ETH. If 8 user deposits yield totalFee = 0.0084 ETH, the fee recipient loses 0.00105 ETH (12.5%) when their fee deposit is processed. At higher basefees (but capped at ethDepositMaxFee = 0.1 ETH), the absolute loss increases. Over the protocol's lifetime, this represents a cumulative misallocation of fees between proposers.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Every proposer who processes ETH deposits and earns a fee deposit will have that fee reduced by a processing fee when their fee deposit is eventually processed. The percentage loss depends on basefee conditions: at 50 Gwei with 8 deposits, ~12.5% loss; with 32 deposits, ~3.1% loss (since totalFee is larger relative to the fixed per-deposit fee). This is a permanent, systematic issue affecting all proposers across the protocol's lifetime.
recommended_mitigations: Process the fee deposit directly on L2 without inserting it into the ring buffer. Instead of writing to ethDeposits and incrementing numEthDeposits, include the fee deposit in the returned deposits_ array with the full totalFee amount.
Alternatively, mark fee deposits distinctly so they are exempt from fee deduction during processing.
Or, add a separate mechanism to credit fee recipients on L2 that bypasses the deposit processing pipeline entirely.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy TaikoL1 with default config. 2. Make 8+ ETH deposits via depositEtherToL2. 3. Propose a block - processDeposits runs, creating a fee deposit at numEthDeposits. 4. Verify numEthDeposits - nextEthDepositToProcess = 1 (fee deposit is pending). 5. Make 7 more ETH deposits so total pending = 8. 6. Propose another block - processDeposits processes all 8 pending including the fee deposit from step 3. 7. Verify the fee deposit had a processing fee deducted from it. 8. Compare the fee recipient's credited amount on L2 with the original totalFee.
uncertainties: The timing of when fee deposits get processed depends on the rate of new user deposits - with low deposit activity, fee deposits may accumulate for a long time before being processed.
Whether this is an intentional design trade-off for simplicity (no separate fee crediting mechanism) vs. an oversight is uncertain from the code alone.
additional_testing_needed: Simulate multiple rounds of processDeposits to measure cumulative fee leakage
Test edge case where fee deposit amount is less than or equal to the processing fee (entire fee is consumed)
Test with varying basefee levels to quantify impact range
Verify that the recursive nature (fee deposit on fee deposit) doesn't cause unexpected accumulation patterns
